details: Bisecting moreh.sum 1D inf regression on astancov/eltwise_run7_refined
head: a7593b9287fa

details: Bug confirmed at HEAD. 1D shapes return wrong values (5→14.5, 16→15.19, 100→70.5). Not all values are inf but they are all BROKEN.
bisect probe at HEAD returns inf — confirmed BROKEN
baseline e2e1cb81e3a (parent of oldest suspect a5b493a70f9) ALSO FAILS — bug pre-exists the suspect window. Need to walk further back.
Bug exists at merge-base f46fbe76303 (main) — this is NOT a branch regression. The Reg B briefing premise is wrong; moreh.sum 1D inf is a long-standing bug in main.

Bug is HOST-SIDE in moreh_sum.cpp:19 — uses padded_shape().rank() instead of logical_shape().rank(). Introduced Jan 23 2025 by commit 0cc4e4eb2f4 (Remove all usages of get_legacy_shape). For 1D inputs with dim=None: padded_shape rank=2 → dims=[0,1] → two redundant reduce calls. dim=0 explicit works correctly. moreh_mean.cpp:20 already uses logical_shape() correctly.

root_cause: moreh_sum.cpp:19 uses padded_shape().rank() instead of logical_shape().rank()
fix: change padded_shape().rank() to logical_shape().rank() — same fix as moreh_mean.cpp:20
verification: dim=0 explicit works correctly; dim=None broken on 1D
